body:
## Problem

Repeated or orchestrated `fix-pr` runs can miss the current orchestrator handoff instructions when the agent session resumes. This can make `fix-pr` no-op even when the preceding review synthesis and orchestrator handoff contain concrete, actionable fixes.

Example from PR #77:

- Review/orchestrator context had concrete fixes to apply: marker source validation, docs correction, terminal-state classification, and regressions.
- The `fix-pr` run still posted: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77#issuecomment-4363352475
- The response said: `No actionable branch work found for this /orchestrate run.`

Run-log clues from the failing `fix-pr` run:

- `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT` contained the concrete fix instructions.
- The full prompt was rendered with a large `prompt_length`, but the resumed run used a tiny continuation prompt.
- Existing `fix-pr` thread state was found and `session_ensure_outcome` was `resumed`.
- The agent behaved as if it was responding to the original `/orchestrate` request rather than the current `fix-pr` handoff.

This suggests route-critical dynamic context such as `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT` is not included in resumed-session continuation prompts.

Related issues:

- #79 tracks what the orchestrator should do after an orchestrated task reports `no_changes`/failure.
- #58 is related background on orchestrator/session continuity.

## Why this matters

The orchestrator can correctly decide `review -> fix-pr` and pass concrete `handoff_context`, but the receiving `fix-pr` agent may not see that context after session resume. This turns actionable review feedback into a false `no_changes` result.

## Potential fixes

One or more of:

1. Include `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT` and other route-critical supplemental variables in the continuation prompt.
2. For `fix-pr`, send the full rendered route prompt whenever `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT` is non-empty.
3. When dispatching orchestrated `fix-pr`, also copy concise handoff instructions into `request_text` so continuation prompts contain the actionable request.
4. Add a route-specific continuation prompt builder instead of using the same minimal metadata prompt for all routes.

## Acceptance criteria

- A resumed `fix-pr` run receives the current actionable orchestrator handoff context.
- A regression test covers resumed `fix-pr` prompt construction with non-empty `ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT`.
- A regression test or fixture verifies that the agent-facing prompt contains enough context to address the latest review synthesis/handoff, not just the original trigger text.
- Existing direct/manual `fix-pr` behavior remains unchanged when no orchestrator context is present.

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-84/codex-25260959858`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/89

Updated the agent runtime so resumed orchestrator-launched fix-pr runs with non-empty handoff context replay the full current fix-pr prompt instead of a minimal continuation prompt; added prompt-continuation regression tests and documented the behavior. Verified with npm --prefix .agent test and a focused rebuild/test.

title: Preserve orchestrator handoff context for resumed fix-pr runs
